Name: Is there morphological marking on the verb dedicated to the expression of apprehension?

Comment: There are only some rudiments, e.g., tä hiit kivi järve (3SG throw.3SG stone.GEN lake.ILL) 's/he will throw the stone into the lake' vs. tä hiitäs pikäle (3SG throw.REFL.3SG down) 's/he lies down'.

Name: Can non-singular 2nd and 3rd person have the same person/number marking or morphosyntactic treatment?
Comment: Only in imperfect forms, where one of the endings in both the second and the third person plural is a glottal stop. In both cases, it is neither the only nor the most frequently used ending, but it is nevertheless in use and can be used with all verbs. Pronoun is mostly dropped. In present tense forms, the glottal stop is not the ending for 2PL and 3PL.

Name: Can the 3rd person plural verb form and impersonal (passive) verb form be encoded in the same way?
Are 2nd person singular verb forms commonly used with a generic meaning?
Can an infinitival verb form be used in a directive function?
Can verbs in purposive subordinate clauses be infinitive forms?
Comment: It is possible for the main verb of a purpose clause to be in the infinitive form, but usually it is a finite verb in indicative or in conditional mood.

Name: Are there sentences without overt verbal arguments?
Is a copula commonly used for predicate nominals in the 3rd person singular forms of the present tense?

Comment: It is common to use a copula, e.g. ma olõ väläh (1SG be[1SG] outside) 'I am outside'. While playing a card game one could also say ma väläh (1SG out) 'I'm out’.
